name: DHT11
tagline: Reads temperature (0–50°C) and humidity (20–80%RH) via single-wire protocol
description: The DHT11 is a basic, low-cost digital temperature and humidity sensor. It uses a capacitive humidity sensor and a thermistor to measure the surrounding air, then outputs a digital signal on the data pin. No analog pins needed. Ideal for beginners building weather stations, smart rooms, or plant monitors.

pinout.name: DATA
pinout.desc: Serial data output (connect to digital pin)

wiringNote: Always add a 10kΩ pull-up resistor between DATA and VCC for stable readings.
libraryInstall: Arduino IDE → Library Manager → Search 'DHT sensor library' → Install
commonMistakes: Forgetting the 10kΩ pull-up resistor → NaN readings
Reading too fast (< 1 sec delay) → sensor fails to respond
Using analog pin instead of digital pin
